merged_events.event_description: First U.S. consul appointed to Rome, establishing a consular presence in the Papal States capital.

evidence.quote: The first U.S. consul at Rome, John Baptiste Sartori, was appointed on June 26, 1797.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Legation established in Rome when Jacob L. Martin presented credentials as the first U.S. Chargé d'Affaires Near the Papal States.

evidence.quote: The U.S. Legation in Rome was established in 1848 when the first U.S. Chargé d'Affaires Jacob L. Martin presented his credentials on August 19, 1848.

merged_events.event_description: The American Legation in Rome closed after Congress refused to fund the mission, ending U.S. diplomatic representation to the Papal States.

evidence.quote: The American mission to the Papal States closed in 1867 after Congress refused to fund the mission any longer.

matched.notes: Exact date and status match. Both refer to the appointment of John Baptiste Sartori as first U.S. consul at Rome on 1797-06-26.
Exact date and status match. Both refer to the establishment of the U.S. Legation when Jacob L. Martin presented credentials on 1848-08-19.
Date match at year-level precision (both 1867) and status match (None). Both refer to the closure of the American mission after Congress refused funding.
